current_fact.fact_text: On April 11, 1957, Britain agreed to grant Singapore internal self-government, setting the stage for the island’s transformation from colonial port into an independent city-state. Within less than a decade, Singapore would go from colony to self-governing state, to part of Malaysia, and then to a fully sovereign nation.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

current_fact.match_reason: Singapore’s journey toward self-rule mirrors a 'Destination' narrative: finding roots, charting new territory, and redefining identity—much like this genre-hopping sonic voyage.

queue.fact_text: On April 11, 1993, a riot erupted at the Southern Ohio Correctional Facility in Lucasville when hundreds of prisoners seized control of cellblocks. The 10-day standoff, sparked in part by objections to mandatory tuberculosis testing for Nation of Islam inmates, became one of the longest and most complex prison uprisings in U.S. history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: A prison uprising over coercion and identity collides thematically with an episode about 'unboxing' and Kendrick Lamar’s reflections on race, control, and self-definition.

queue.fact_text: On April 11, 1987, Israel’s foreign minister Shimon Peres and Jordan’s King Hussein secretly signed the London Agreement, sketching a framework for an international peace conference on the Arab–Israeli conflict. Though it ultimately unraveled, it showed how much of peacemaking happens quietly, far from microphones and headlines.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: A secret, fragile attempt at peace suits the hushed, downtempo atmosphere of "Slow Planet"—both about slowing down heated conflict and seeking calm, deliberate dialogue.

queue.fact_text: On April 11, 1981, simmering tensions between police and Black residents in Brixton, south London, exploded into a major riot. The clashes, sparked by aggressive stop-and-search operations, left hundreds injured and forced Britain to confront deep questions about race, policing, and urban inequality.

episode.title: Morning Drops #32 - A Weekend In London
episode.description: Recorded in London, marking a weekend reunion between Gaia and Yasmin. A selection inspired by London's energy, seen through the eyes of two inseparable friends — music from concerts, club nights, and long walks across town. Four tracks to ease you into the week at the right pace.
songs: Jimmy Castor Bunch - It's Just Begun
The Asphodells - Late Flowering Lust (Phil Kieran Remix)

queue.match_reason: A landmark London riot rooted in race and policing pairs with a London-focused episode that includes Little Simz, a contemporary voice from the city’s Black communities, reflecting how its energy and struggles live on in music.

queue.fact_text: Another layer of the April 11, 1993 Lucasville uprising is how prisoners from very different groups—Black Muslims, white supremacists, and others—formed a temporary alliance to negotiate over shared grievances. For 10 tense days, this unlikely coalition controlled large parts of the prison, revealing both fracture and fragile solidarity behind the walls.

episode.title: Sedna FM #06 - Alters of an Ego
episode.description: What if you could step outside yourself and become someone freer, bolder, or completely unexpected? Exploring the artistry of musicians who craft alter-egos and parallel realities to experiment with new dimensions of their identity. It's not about hiding who they are — it's about expanding who they can be.
songs: The Fireman - Strawberries Oceans Ships Forest

queue.match_reason: The uprising’s shifting identities, alliances, and roles echo an episode about alter-egos and parallel selves—people temporarily becoming something else to survive a pressure-cooker reality.

queue.fact_text: A lesser-noticed thread in the story of the 2012 Indian Ocean earthquakes is how they revealed a 'tectonic reorganization'—evidence that the Indo-Australian plate is slowly cracking into separate Indian and Australian plates. The quakes didn’t just shake the ocean; they showed that even the planet’s crust is in the middle of a long, slow identity shift.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: An oceanic plate quietly splitting beneath the seafloor fits an episode about going below the surface—immersing into hidden currents and long, fluid transformations.

queue.fact_text: When Bach’s St Matthew Passion premiered in 1727, its scale—double choir, double orchestra, and a rich tapestry of soloists—was unusually vast for a church work. For some Leipzig listeners, it was less a pious backdrop and more an overwhelming, immersive soundworld inside their local church.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

queue.match_reason: A monumental, enduring sacred work aligns with an episode about timeless musical legacies—pieces that, like Bach’s Passion, continue to resonate across centuries.

queue.fact_text: The April 11, 1970 launch of Apollo 13 carried a small, poetic detail: the Saturn V’s third stage later went into a solar orbit, quietly circling the Sun long after the drama of the mission ended. While the crew struggled to survive, a discarded piece of their rocket became just another object drifting through the cosmic neighborhood.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description:
Cosmic Monday is a Morning Drops episode built around four Italian dance records, tracing how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language.
Gaia moves through four tracks like chapters: groove first, then machines, then that bright, futuristic feeling you only get when the bassline is doing the talking. It's not nostalgia, exactly. It's more like returning to a sound that still works — especially on mornings when you need a little extra energy, but you don't want anything aggressive.
Take it as a slow transmission. Let it carry you into the day.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

queue.match_reason: A spent rocket stage orbiting the Sun is a literal piece of cosmic debris, well-suited to an episode called "Cosmic Monday" that celebrates futurism, machines, and space-tinged dance music.

published.fact_text: In the depths of the Indian Ocean's Wharton Basin on April 11, 2012, a rare 'doublet' of great strike-slip earthquakes ruptured within hours of each other, each exceeding magnitude 8. They twisted the seafloor sideways rather than thrusting it upward, generating only a small, non-destructive tsunami despite their immense power.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

published.match_reason: A deep-ocean seismic doublet pairs with underwater, cinematic trip-hop textures like "Underwater Love," evoking tectonic shifts beneath the surface.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1970, Apollo 13 launched toward the Moon in what was supposed to be NASA's third lunar landing, carrying astronauts Jim Lovell, Jack Swigert, and Fred Haise. The mission would soon become a legendary story of survival, but at liftoff it was pure optimism: a Saturn V roaring skyward, humanity reaching again for another world.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A mission to space fits naturally with an episode built around "Space Shift" and songs about new beginnings and bold trajectories like "Go" and "You Only Live Once."

published.fact_text: On April 11, 2001, Australia's men's national soccer team defeated American Samoa 31–0, setting the record for the largest victory in an international football match. Striker Archie Thompson scored 13 goals in that single game, a world record for most goals by one player in an international fixture.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

published.match_reason: A wild, record-breaking 31–0 game mirrors the high-energy, forward-driving momentum of an "On The Go" mix full of funk, reggae, and kinetic grooves.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1814, the Treaty of Fontainebleau forced Napoleon to abdicate the French throne unconditionally and go into exile on Elba. It marked the dramatic fall of one of history's most ambitious rulers, temporarily ending years of Napoleonic warfare that had reshaped Europe.

episode.title: Morning Drops #46 - Fog and Fire
episode.description: In today’s Morning Drops, Gaia brings us on a passport-free trip between two Italian energies: the foggy stillness of the North and the volcanic pulse of the South. Throughout the journey, Gaia shares four of her favourite Italian songwriters. Grab your coffee and tune into the radio show from another planet!
songs: Edoardo Bennato - La Torre Di Babele
Lucio Dalla - Com'è Profondo il Mare

published.match_reason: The downfall of an emperor and a continent in turmoil resonates with songs about towers of Babel, inner devils, and deep seas—"fog and fire" as a metaphor for empires rising and collapsing.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1727, Johann Sebastian Bach’s monumental St Matthew Passion was first performed at St. Thomas Church in Leipzig. Combining intricate choruses, solo arias, and haunting chorales, it wove theology, drama, and emotion into one of the most profound works in Western sacred music.

episode.title: Sedna FM #04 - Queen of Spades
episode.description: Tuning in from the planet of irrational love. Exploring love's most vulnerable side — the kind that awakens the child within us. A diverse music selection ranging from electric blues to downtempo, soul, and jazz. A tribute to the feeling that has moved artists to create the most beautiful works. Love makes us vulnerable, and there is no art without vulnerability.
songs: Alabama Shakes - Gimme All Your Love
Queens of the stone age - Make it wit chu
Etta James - I'd Rather Go Blind
Eric Clapton - Have you ever loved a woman

published.match_reason: Bach’s Passion is a towering work of devotion and vulnerability, fitting an episode that celebrates love, longing, and the emotional intensity that fuels great art.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 2006, Iranian president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad announced that Iran had successfully enriched uranium, declaring the country had joined the 'nuclear club.' Behind the political controversy was a complex story of physics, centrifuge cascades, and the global tension around atomic technology.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

published.match_reason: A milestone in nuclear technology echoes an episode reflecting on our ambivalent relationship with powerful technologies—from atoms to algorithms and the systems that quietly shape our lives.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1908, the German armored cruiser SMS Blücher was launched, the last of her kind built for the Imperial German Navy. Designed just as naval warfare was shifting toward faster, more heavily gunned battlecruisers, she became a symbol of a rapidly changing technological era at sea.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: A German warship launched into the sea connects playfully with a modern, peaceful voyage "Sailing With Germans," turning naval steel into sunlit waves and songs.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1809, during the Battle of the Basque Roads, daring British captain Lord Cochrane led a fire-ship attack that trapped much of the French fleet — but his commanding admiral, Lord Gambier, hesitated to fully press the advantage. The result was a partial, controversial victory that sparked a political storm back in Britain.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

published.match_reason: A bold but under-supported naval raid pairs neatly with "Ma come fanno i marinai" (How Do Sailors Do It), tying historical seamanship to modern songs of the sea and courage.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1945, American forces liberated the Buchenwald concentration camp near Weimar, Germany, uncovering the brutal reality of Nazi persecution. Survivors had already organized a resistance committee inside the camp, and some had even seized control of parts of Buchenwald before the U.S. troops arrived.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

published.match_reason: The liberation of Buchenwald demands space for remembrance and healing; a reflective episode about pain, recovery, and looking back with awareness offers a respectful emotional frame.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1935, Britain, France, and Italy opened the Stresa Front conference on the shores of Lake Maggiore, hoping to present a united front against Nazi Germany’s treaty violations. Within months, shifting interests and Mussolini’s invasion of Ethiopia would quietly fracture this short-lived attempt at European solidarity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

published.match_reason: A lakeside summit about fragile alliances pairs with a travel-themed, perspective-shifting mix—both about crossing borders, rethinking positions, and moving through uncertainty.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1856, in the Second Battle of Rivas, Costa Rican drummer Juan Santamaría volunteered to set fire to a hostel packed with the forces of American filibuster William Walker. He was killed in the act, but his daring move forced Walker’s men to abandon the position and became a foundational legend of Costa Rican national identity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

published.match_reason: A Latin American tale of sacrifice and anti-imperialist resistance connects strongly with Residente’s politically charged, identity-focused storytelling across the Spanish-speaking world.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 2023, a Myanmar Air Force airstrike on the village of Pazigyi in Sagaing Region killed at least 100 civilians attending a local gathering. The attack became one of the deadliest incidents in Myanmar’s post-coup conflict, highlighting the devastating human cost of air power turned against its own population.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: An airstrike massacre is a heavy storm in human form; pairing it with a reflective episode about enduring dark weather and holding onto hope creates room for mourning and cautious optimism.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1993, anti-fascist Catalan activist Guillem Agulló was murdered by a group of Spanish neo-Nazis while on holiday in Montanejos. His death became a rallying point for anti-fascist and pro-Catalan movements, turning a personal tragedy into a symbol of ongoing resistance against hate.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: The politically charged murder of an anti-fascist activist fits the uncompromising, protest-driven roar of Rage Against the Machine and an episode dedicated to resistance.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1990, customs officers in Middlesbrough, England, intercepted what they suspected to be part of a 'supergun' bound for Iraq. The seized steel tube was linked to Project Babylon, an Iraqi plan to build a gigantic long-range cannon that sounded like science fiction but was chillingly real.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

published.match_reason: A clandestine 'supergun' called Project Babylon aligns with an episode titled "November Bomb"—both touching on explosive power and the tension between invention and destruction.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1965, a ferocious Palm Sunday tornado outbreak ripped across six Midwestern U.S. states, spawning 55 tornadoes in less than 12 hours. Many were violent F4 twisters, leaving 266 people dead and entire communities shredded in a matter of minutes.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: A massive tornado outbreak is nature’s own electric shock—unpredictable, high-voltage, and devastating—mirroring the intense, guitar-charged energy of this rock-heavy episode.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1964, Brazilian Marshal Humberto de Alencar Castelo Branco was elected president by Congress, formalizing the new military regime that had just toppled Brazil’s civilian government. His tenure helped entrench a two-decade dictatorship under the banner of 'order and progress.'

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

published.match_reason: A military regime claiming to bring 'order' contrasts sharply with an episode about open seas and shared coasts, underscoring how borders and power can clash with the fluidity of culture and movement.

published.fact_text: On April 11, 1957, Britain agreed to grant Singapore internal self-government, setting the stage for the island’s transformation from colonial port into an independent city-state. Within less than a decade, Singapore would go from colony to self-governing state, to part of Malaysia, and then to a fully sovereign nation.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

published.match_reason: Singapore’s journey toward self-rule mirrors a 'Destination' narrative: finding roots, charting new territory, and redefining identity—much like this genre-hopping sonic voyage.
